political.s: American Express leans right with FEC data showing $690K raised with 22,500 out of 34,000 (66%) going to Republican candidates. Company actively engages in political advocacy and lobbying through PAC and direct contributions.

charity.s: American Express has contributed over $1 billion to charitable causes since 1954. Focuses on education, workforce development, small business support, historic preservation, and disaster relief through its Foundation and Community Impact programs.
charity.focus: Education, workforce development, small business, historic preservation, disaster relief, DEI initiatives

environment.s: Company has environmental commitments within ESG strategy including climate-related risk management and carbon footprint tracking tools for corporate clients. Advancing Climate Solutions is listed as core pillar, though specific reduction targets and third-party certifications are not prominently disclosed.

labor.s: Company reports 91% employee satisfaction and emphasizes workplace culture. However, a whistleblower retaliation case (Sophia Lewis, 2025) alleges harassment and wrongful termination after reporting alleged fraudulent practices, indicating potential labor compliance issues.

dei.s: American Express integrates DE&I as fundamental part of ESG strategy with published workforce EEO-1 data and leadership from Chief DE&I Officer. Company emphasizes transparent recruitment, diverse candidate development, and board-level accountability with 15% of annual scorecard tied to diversity goals.

animals.s: Not applicable to financial services company.

guns.s: Not applicable to financial services company.

privacy.s: American Express maintains comprehensive privacy principles including GDPR compliance and data protection standards. However, company collects extensive transaction and spending pattern data, shares with partners, and allows extensive marketing use of personal information despite privacy controls offered.

execPay.s: CEO Stephen Squeri earned $46.24M in 2025 total compensation (up 24% YoY), comprising 96.3% variable pay. CEO-to-employee pay ratio stands at 814:1 according to 2025 data, significantly above average even for financial services industry standards.

fec.committeeName: AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (AXPPAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
